2606.30219.title: EvalSafetyGap: A Hybrid Survey and Conceptual Framework for LLM Evaluation-Safety Failures
2606.30219.paper_summary: LLM evaluation and AI safety face a shared measurement problem: benchmark scores, reward-model signals, and reported safety metrics can improve while the latent properties they are meant to represent remain difficult to verify. This paper combines a hybrid survey - a systematic search paired with narrative synthesis and separately tracked grey evidence - with a conceptual framework and a structured ten-model audit. The synthesis spans eight evidence streams: benchmark validity, dynamic evaluation, LLM-as-judge reliability, safety evaluation, jailbreak/refusal robustness, reward hacking, mechanistic interpretability, and governance/auditability, covering 2018-2026 evaluation-safety measurement work. We introduce EvalSafetyGap as an organizing hypothesis for comparing evaluation-side and alignment-side proxy failures under optimization pressure, using Goodhart's Law together with two constructs we develop here - an Instability Decomposition and an Alignment Trilemma - as tools for generating testable comparisons. The audit shows how conclusions shift when capability, behavioral safety, and governance are measured separately. In this sample (n = 10), the association between capability and sustained adversarial robustness is statistically indeterminate using the displayed Table 3 inputs (Pearson r = +0.232, p = 0.520), and the apparent open-closed safety gap is modest, driven mainly by governance and disclosure rather than behavioral robustness, and sensitive to how a single borderline model is classified; attempt-budget results are protocol dependent. Because the public evidence uses heterogeneous protocols, the audit is diagnostic rather than rank-generating. The contribution is a shared vocabulary and evidence map to support dynamic evaluation, transparent source reporting, multi-attempt safety measurement, and auditable alignment practice.
2606.30219.paper_summary_zh: LLM 評估和 AI 安全面臨著共同的測量問題：基準分數、獎勵模型信號和報告的安全指標可能會改善，而它們所代表的潛在特性仍然難以驗證。本文結合了一種混合調查——系統性搜索與敘述綜合相結合，並單獨跟踪灰色證據——以及一個概念框架和結構化的十模型審計。這一綜合涵蓋了八個證據流：基準有效性、動態評估、LLM 作為評判者的可靠性、安全評估、越獄/拒絕的穩健性、獎勵駭客、機械可解釋性，以及治理/可審計性，涵蓋了 2018-2026 年的評估-安全測量工作。我們引入 EvalSafetyGap 作為一個組織假設，用於比較在優化壓力下評估側和對齊側的代理失敗，使用古德哈特法則以及我們在此開發的兩個構造——不穩定性分解和對齊三難——作為生成可測試比較的工具。審計顯示當能力、行為安全和治理單獨測量時，結論如何轉變。在這個樣本中（n = 10），使用顯示的表 3 輸入，能力與持續對抗穩健性之間的關聯在統計上是不確定的（Pearson r = +0.232, p = 0.520），而明顯的開放-封閉安全差距是適度的，主要由治理和披露驅動，而非行為穩健性，並且對如何對單一邊界模型進行分類敏感；嘗試-預算結果依賴於協議。由於公共證據使用異質協議，審計是診斷性的，而非排名生成的。貢獻是一個共享的詞彙和證據地圖，以支持動態評估、透明的來源報告、多次嘗試的安全測量和可審計的對齊實踐。
2606.30219.author: Buğra Alperen Uluırmak et.al.
